qas.a: Used to assign and track personnel based on their skills and knowledge
An alphanumeric code consisting of 5 characters describing various AF jobs
1st character of AFSE
identifies the AFSC in terms of its relationship to the AF Mission.

qas.q: Career Groups divided into the following categories
qas.a:
1 Operation
2 Maintenance& Logistics
3 Support
4 Medical& Dental
5 Legal & Chaplain
6 Finance & Contracting
7 Special Investigations
AFSC's 1st and 2nd character of AFSC (3D).
Indicates a functional grouping of personnel requiring basically the same knowledge and skills.
Perform tasks as a Cyber Systems Operations Specialist for the AF and other Department of Defense
1st thru 3rd characters of AFSC (3D0).
identifies a grouping of personnel with related work requirements and qualifications.

Is divided into two groups:
- Operations (3D0)
- Systems (3D1).

qas.q: The 3D0s specialties in the Operations career fields are:
qas.a:
- Knowledge Operations Management
- Cyber Systems Operations
- Cyber Surety
- Computer Systems Programming
4th character of AFSC identifies
It specifies the specific degree of experience an individual must obtains within the particular AFSC.
NOT an automatic process. Each level is earned based on experience, training and rank.

Apprentice
- Graduate Technical Training School

Helper
- Graduate Basic Military Training School
Journeyman

Complete the following:
- 5-level (CDC)
- Computer Based Training (CBT)
- Air Force Job Qualification Standard (AFJQS)
- Air Force Qualification Training Package (AFQTP) for equipment at assigned location
Craftsman

- Sew on SSgt
- 12 months OJT
- 6 months OJT for (re-trainees)
- 7-level Training and CBT
- E6CW3DX7X 01AA Cyberspace Career Advancement Courses
- AFQTP 3DXXX-232A, Communications and Information Work Center Supervisor's Handbook.
- AFJQSs/AFQTPs for equipment at assigned

Superintendent
- Sew on SMSgt
- Complete E6ACW3DX9X 00AA, Cyberspace Superintendent Course
- Complete specific AFJQSs/AFQTPs for equipment

5th character

(Example: 3D032 identifies Cyber Systems Operations).
AF system for selecting Airman for rank promotion to Staff Sergeant (E-5) and Technical Sergeant (E-6).

qas.q: WAPS comprised of what four factors
qas.a:
- Specialty Knowledge Test (SKT)
- Promotion Fitness Examination (PFE)
- Enlisted Performance Report (EPR)
- Military Decorations and Awards

qas.q: Eligiblility for promotion is based on
qas.a:
- Time-in-Service (TIS),
- Time-in-Grade (TIG)
- Skill Level

- up to 100 points
- measures specific job information
- based on CDC

- up to 100 points
- measures general military knowledge in a specific grade level
- questions are taken from the AF Handbook 1

- up to 250 points
- measures Airman's past job performance and promotion potential
- up to 25 points

1. On-The-Job Training (OJT) - at the work site
2. Computer-Based Training (CBT) - on a computer system.
3. Career Development Course (CDC)
4. Air Force Job Qualification Standard (AFJQS)
5. Air Force Qualification Training Package (AFQTP)

Comprehensive task list describing a particular job type or duty position for all in the position.

Supervisors use to document task qualification
Instructional course designed for use at the unit to qualify or aid qualification in a duty position, program or on a piece of equipment.
Manage the AF Specialty (AFS)
Are responsible for creating the Career Field Education and Training Plan (CFETP).

qas.q: Career Field Education and Training Plan (CFETP)
qas.a:
Comprehensive core training document identifying life-cycle training
education requirements
support resources
minimum core task requirements for an AFSC.

qas.q: Career Field Education and Training Plan (CFETP)
qas.a: The CFETP document contains 3 sections

• Server operating systems
• Database administration
• Web technologies
• Project management
• Computer operator supervisors
• Computer-based consultant

• System and User Accounts Services
• Security
• Contingency Operations
• Strategic and Budget Planning

qas.q: (3D0X2 Responsibilites) - System and User Accounts Services
qas.a:
Responsible for managing system accounts.
- Directory services
- assigning dynamic IP addresses
- Domain Name Server (DNS),
- Storage Area Network (SAN)
- E-mail

A key responsibility to implement security policies, fixes, updates, OS patches and antivirus software.

- DISA provides Information Assurance Vulnerability Assessments (IAVA) for security weaknesses

Test, Tactics, Techniques and Procedures (TTPs) in both defensive and offensive missions

identify and analyze targets
exploits adversary's network operations/systems

- Planning and implementation activities for network
- Purchasing equipment/software
- Manage infrastucture installations

- AF Intranet Control (AFINC)
- AF Cyber Security and Control System (CSCS)
Utilized to provide 24/7 network operations and management functions.
Top level boundary and entry point into the AF Information Network (AFIN)
full spectrum network management and network defense for the AF provisioned portion of the Global Information Grid (GIG).
Controls the flow of all external and inter-base traffic through a standard, centralized managed gateway.
Provides 24/7 network operations, management functions and enable key enterprise services within AF unclassified and classified networks.
Information Assurance (IA) strategywith multiple layers of defense placed throughout an information system.
Seeks to make system penetration difficult at every layer of access
Standards and policies to protect networks, and reducing maintenance, down-time, costs, and training.
Ability to identify threats and take countermeasures against them before the integrity of system is compromised.
